key_claims.claim: Severe brain injury after FLA for TTTS evolves from early periventricular hemorrhagic infarction to late cerebral volume loss and polymicrogyria
Gene panels would capture approximately 88 percent of pathogenic variants identified by exome sequencing for fetal anomalies, with ES adding most value in multisystem and cardiac/CNS cases
Maternal sirolimus is a feasible prenatal therapy for fetal kaposiform hemangioendothelioma with Kasabach-Merritt phenomenon
Transcardiac transarterial embolization for vein of Galen malformation is technically feasible in a fetal lamb model
CMA diagnostic yield is approximately 5 percent in isolated severe early-onset FGR, with minimal added yield from exome or genome sequencing
